title: Standardize CLI: add podman quadlet cat alias/subcommand to match systemctl cat
body:
### Feature request description

Dear team:
Request to add a 'podman quadlet cat' command (or alias) that behaves like 'podman quadlet print', to align Podman's quadlet CLI with systemd conventions and improve discoverability for users familiar with 'systemctl cat unit' command.

### Suggest potential solution

Add 'podman quadlet cat' as either:
- A full subcommand that is functionally identical to podman quadlet print, or
- A simple alias/hidden wrapper that calls print.

Behavior requirements:
- Exact same output and flags as podman quadlet print.
- Exit codes identical to print.
- Preserve existing help text semantics.


### Have you considered any alternatives?

The proposal is an alternative for podman the 'quadlet print' command.

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body: So an alias of `podman quadlet print` to `podman quadlet cat`? That sounds reasonable.

Is just adding an alias like `Aliases: []string{"cat"}` enough in [`quadlet/print.go`](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/090304a054d11215ad5c455e0dc43dcf62ebd06c/cmd/podman/quadlet/print.go#L14-L24)?
You'll also need to make a small edit to the manpage to add the alias there, but the core of this is adding the alias, yes
